topic: You are moving ~828,000 km/h through the galaxy while apparently sitting perfectly still.

beats.narration: Right now, sitting perfectly still, you feel like nothing is moving. That feeling is the greatest illusion of your life.
beats.intended_understanding: YOU, right now, apparently motionless, are the subject — establish apparent stillness before revealing hidden motion.

beats.narration: We think we know what fast means. Sprinter 44, jet 900, bullet 3,400 km/h.
beats.intended_understanding: anchor everyday 'fast' speeds so the reveal later dwarfs them

beats.narration: But the ground beneath your feet is not still. Earth spins at 1,670 km/h at the equator.
beats.intended_understanding: the ground you stand on is itself moving fast

beats.narration: Half the speed of a bullet, and you never feel it.
beats.intended_understanding: the spin speed vs the bullet

beats.narration: Now pull back and watch what our whole planet is doing.
beats.intended_understanding: zoom out from the spin to the orbit

beats.narration: Earth falls around the Sun at 107,000 km/h, 30 km every second.
And the Sun itself is not still; it falls around the galaxy dragging every planet.
beats.intended_understanding: even the Sun/solar system is moving
beats.visual: full narration sentence on empty background

beats.intended_understanding: the headline number — our galactic speed
beats.visual: 828,000 number burned over Milky Way footage

beats.narration: A tiny world, riding a star, around a galaxy itself racing through the universe.
beats.intended_understanding: our smallness / scale of the galaxy

beats.narration: You have never been still, not for one single second of your life.
beats.intended_understanding: THE conceptual payoff — the whole thesis lands
beats.visual: full narration sentence on empty background

beats.narration: The stillness you feel isn't stillness at all; it's the speed of everything moving together.
